label: 模擬試題7
source: Vocatopia 模擬試題（比照 2026 會考架構，全文字版，用字限會考核心字）

items.stem: Amy is very ＿＿＿. She always finishes her work before the deadline and never makes mistakes.
items.explanation: 準時完成工作且不犯錯代表做事細心，故選 careful（細心的）。

items.stem: The library is very quiet, so please keep your voice ＿＿＿ when you talk to your friends there.
items.explanation: 圖書館需要安靜，說話要 keep your voice low（放低音量），故選 (B)。

items.stem: This math problem is too difficult for me. Can you give me a ＿＿＿ before I give up?
items.explanation: 解不出數學題時需要的是 hint（提示），其餘選項與解題無關，故選 (A)。

items.stem: Tom ＿＿＿ breakfast at seven every morning before he goes to school.
items.explanation: 主詞 Tom 為第三人稱單數，every morning 表習慣性動作，用現在簡單式加 -s，故選 eats。

items.stem: A: ＿＿＿ your parents at home now? B: No, they are both at work.
items.explanation: 回答用 they are，可知問句用 be 動詞；主詞 your parents 為複數，故選 Are。

items.stem: Look at those dark clouds in the sky. It ＿＿＿ rain very soon.
items.explanation: 看到烏雲這個徵兆表示即將發生的事，用 be going to 表近未來，故選 (C)。

items.stem: Of the three paintings on the wall, this one is the ＿＿＿ beautiful, in my opinion.
items.explanation: of the three 表在三者以上的範圍中做比較，須用最高級 the most beautiful，故選 (B)。

items.stem: I forgot to bring a pen today. Could you ＿＿＿ me yours for the test, please?
items.explanation: 「借某人某物」用 lend somebody something；borrow 是「向他人借」，方向相反，故選 lend。

items.stem: Ellen was extremely nervous ＿＿＿ she had never given a speech in public before.
items.explanation: 後句「從未在公開場合演講過」是前句「緊張」的原因，故用 because 引導原因子句。

items.stem: The man ＿＿＿ car was stolen last night reported the case to the police early this morning.
items.explanation: 空格後接名詞 car，且表示「他的車」，須用所有格關係代名詞 whose，故選 (C)。

items.stem: This old stone bridge ＿＿＿ over a hundred years ago, and it is still standing strong today.
items.explanation: 橋是「被建造」的，且 a hundred years ago 表過去，須用過去被動 was built，故選 (C)。

items.stem: My little sister is still afraid of ＿＿＿ alone in the dark, so she always keeps a small light on.
items.explanation: be afraid of 為介系詞片語，後面須接動名詞（V-ing），故選 staying。

items.stem: You ＿＿＿ finish this report by Friday; the manager needs it for an important meeting.
items.explanation: 經理需要這份報告開會，語意上表達強烈的義務，故用 must（必須）。

items.stem: By the time we arrived at the station, the last train ＿＿＿ already.
items.explanation: by the time + 過去式子句，表在過去某時間點之前已完成的動作，須用過去完成式 had left。

items.stem: The haunted house at the fair was so ＿＿＿ that several visitors screamed and ran back outside.
items.explanation: 遊客因害怕而尖叫逃出，代表鬼屋很 frightening（令人害怕的），故選 (B)。

items.stem: You had better ＿＿＿ an umbrella with you; the weather report says it will rain later this afternoon.
items.explanation: had better 後面固定接原形動詞，故選 bring。

items.stem: The coach told the players ＿＿＿ harder if they really wanted to win the championship this year.
items.explanation: tell somebody to do something 為固定句型，須用不定詞 to practice，故選 (C)。

items.stem: Neither the teacher nor the students ＿＿＿ ready for the sudden fire drill this morning.
items.explanation: neither A nor B 的動詞須與最接近的主詞一致，此處最近為 the students（複數），故用過去式 were。

items.stem: The harder you work on your studies now, the ＿＿＿ chance you will have of passing the exam.
items.explanation: 「the + 比較級, the + 比較級」句型，兩邊皆用比較級，故選 better。

passages.title: Rainbow Land Amusement Park — Ticket & Ride Guide
passages.passage:
Rainbow Land Amusement Park — Ticket & Ride Guide

Ticket Prices: Adult (age 13 and up) $500 / Child (age 4-12) $350 / Children under 4 enter free.

Ride Height Rules:
Sky Wheel — no height limit.
Thunder Coaster — riders must be at least 140 cm tall. Riders between 140 cm and 150 cm must ride together with an adult.
Splash Boat — riders must be at least 120 cm tall.

Family Package: Buy two adult tickets together at the ticket counter, and two child tickets in the same order will be sold at half price. This offer is only available at the counter and cannot be used for online orders.

Park Hours: 9:00 a.m.–6:00 p.m., open every day except Tuesday.

questions.stem: The Chen family, two parents and two children (ages 9 and 6), bought tickets together at the counter using the Family Package. How much did they pay in total?
Lily is 145 cm tall. She wants to ride the Thunder Coaster by herself, without any adult beside her. Based on the rules above, can she do this?
options: Yes, because she is taller than 140 cm.
No, because she must ride with an adult until she is 150 cm tall.
No, because the ride is closed to children.
Yes, because there is no height limit for this ride.
optionsZh: 不行，因為未滿150公分前須有大人陪同。
不行，因為這項設施禁止兒童搭乘。
可以，因為此設施沒有身高限制。
questions.explanation: 規則需同時比對兩項：她身高145公分符合「至少140公分」的基本門檻，但同時落在「140–150公分須與大人同行」的範圍內，故不能單獨搭乘，選 (B)。

passages.title: The Woman Next Door
passages.passage:
Amy: Hey Ben, do you know old Mrs. Lin? She lives right next door to me.
Ben: You mean the woman who never says hello to anyone? Most kids on our street think she's kind of unfriendly, maybe even a little scary.
Amy: That's what everyone believes, but nobody has ever knocked on her door to find out. Nobody really tried.
Ben: So why are you smiling when you talk about her now?
Amy: Well, one winter afternoon I saw her struggling to carry a heavy bag of rice up the icy steps to her house. I ran over without thinking and offered to help.
Ben: What happened after that?
Amy: She looked surprised at first, but then she quietly thanked me and invited me in for a cup of tea. Her small house was full of old photographs, and it smelled like warm ginger tea.
Ben: Did she tell you anything about herself?
Amy: Yes. She said her husband had passed away five years earlier, and her children now live far away in another city. She eats dinner alone every night, but she never once complains about it.
Ben: That sounds really lonely.
Amy: Exactly. So after that day, I started stopping by once a week just to chat or bring her some fruit from our garden. She even taught me how to fold paper cranes and told me stories about her youth.
Ben: I guess she just needed someone to notice her.
Amy: That's exactly it. The woman everyone called unfriendly was actually just lonely, quietly waiting for someone to care.

questions.stem: Why did other kids never knock on Mrs. Lin's door?
options: She had asked them not to visit.
Their parents told them not to.
questions.explanation: Ben 說 kids on our street think she's kind of unfriendly, maybe even a little scary，故選 (B)。

questions.stem: What does the dialogue suggest Mrs. Lin was really feeling?
questions.explanation: Amy 最後指出她其實只是 lonely, quietly waiting for someone to care，故選 (C)。

questions.stem: According to the dialogue, which of the following is NOT mentioned?
options: Mrs. Lin's husband died five years ago.
Amy helped Mrs. Lin carry a bag of rice.
Mrs. Lin taught Amy how to fold paper cranes.
Mrs. Lin's children visit her every weekend.
optionsZh: 林太太的先生五年前過世了。
艾咪幫林太太搬了一袋米。
林太太的孩子每週都會來看她。
questions.explanation: 對話中提到林太太的孩子 live far away in another city（住在遙遠的城市），並未提到孩子每週來看她，反而與此矛盾，故選 (D)。

passages.title: How Does a Rainbow Form?
passages.passage:
After a rain shower, when the sun suddenly breaks through the clouds, you may catch sight of a beautiful rainbow stretching across the sky. But what actually makes this colorful arc appear?

A rainbow forms when sunlight passes through countless tiny drops of water still floating in the air. As light enters each raindrop, it bends slightly and splits into different colors. This is much like light passing through a glass prism. The light then bounces off the inside of the drop and bends again as it exits. This spreads the colors even further apart before they reach our eyes.

This is also why a rainbow only appears under certain conditions. You must have your back to the sun while raindrops hang in the air in front of you. Otherwise, the angle will not be right for the colors to reach your eyes. That is why rainbows often appear low in the sky, right after a storm passes and the sun reappears.

Every rainbow always shows the same order of colors, from red on the outer edge to violet on the inner edge. This happens because each color bends at a slightly different angle. Interestingly, no two people ever see the exact same rainbow, since each person's eyes catch light from a slightly different set of raindrops. In a way, every rainbow you see truly belongs only to you.

questions.stem: What causes a raindrop to split sunlight into different colors?
options: The raindrop bends and separates the light.
The raindrop reflects light back to the sun.
The wind mixes the colors together.
The clouds create colors on their own.
optionsZh: 水滴使光線彎折並分散。
questions.explanation: 文中 it bends slightly and splits into different colors，故選 (A)。

questions.stem: Why must a person stand with their back to the sun to see a rainbow?
options: So the sun will not hurt their eyes.
So the light can reach their eyes at the correct angle.
So the raindrops will not fall on them.
So the sky looks darker behind them.
optionsZh: 才不會被太陽刺傷眼睛。
光才能以正確角度到達眼睛。
背後的天空才會顯得較暗。
questions.explanation: 文中 otherwise, the angle will not be right for the colors to reach your eyes，故選 (B)。

questions.stem: What does the reading say about how people see rainbows?
options: Everyone sees the exact same rainbow.
Each person actually sees a slightly different rainbow.
Only certain people can see rainbows.
Rainbows look different depending on the season.
optionsZh: 每個人看到的彩虹完全相同。
每個人看到的彩虹其實略有不同。
只有特定的人看得到彩虹。
questions.explanation: 文末 no two people ever see the exact same rainbow，故選 (B)。

passages.title: Looking Down Without Fear
passages.passage:
Ever since he was a little boy, Jason had been terrified of heights. Even standing on a chair to change a light bulb made his knees shake and his palms sweat. His classmates loved climbing trees and riding roller coasters, but Jason always found an excuse to stay safely on the ground.

Everything changed when his school planned a class trip that included a rope course built high between the trees. Jason wanted to skip it, but his best friend, Amy, refused to let him give up so easily. She stood beside him at the bottom of the tall ladder and said, "You don't have to look down. Just look at me instead."

With his heart pounding, Jason slowly climbed up, gripping the rope tightly with both hands. Halfway across the first wooden bridge, he froze completely, unable to move forward or backward. Amy kept talking to him calmly from below, reminding him to breathe and take one small step at a time. Step by step, he finally reached the other side.

When Jason's feet touched solid ground again, he could not stop smiling. His fear had not disappeared completely, but for the first time, he understood that fear did not have to control every decision he made. He still avoided the very highest platforms, but he no longer let fear decide everything for him.

questions.stem: How did Jason usually react to activities involving heights?
options: He enjoyed them more than his classmates.
He was forced to join by his teacher.
He never noticed the height at all.
optionsZh: 他比同學更喜歡這些活動。
questions.explanation: 文中 Jason always found an excuse to stay safely on the ground，故選 (B)。

questions.stem: What did Amy do to help Jason on the rope course?
options: She carried him across the bridge.
She told the teacher to cancel the activity.
She encouraged him with calm words.
She climbed the ladder for him.
optionsZh: 她用平靜的話語鼓勵他。
questions.explanation: 文中 Amy kept talking to him calmly from below, reminding him to breathe，故選 (C)。

questions.stem: What did Jason realize after finishing the rope course?
options: He would never be afraid again.
Fear did not have to control his decisions.
Heights were not dangerous at all.
He should try even higher platforms next time.
optionsZh: 恐懼不必主宰他的決定。
他下次該挑戰更高的平台。
questions.explanation: 文末 he understood that fear did not have to control every decision he made，故選 (B)。

passages.title: The Long Ride to the Modern Bicycle
passages.passage:
The bicycle we ride today looks simple, but it took nearly a century of trial and error to develop. The earliest version, invented in the early 1800s, had no pedals at all. Riders sat on a wooden frame and pushed the ground with their feet to move forward. This was much like walking while sitting down.

Pedals were not added until decades later, and even then, the design created a new problem. Because the pedals were attached directly to the front wheel, inventors made that wheel bigger and bigger. This allowed the bicycle to travel farther with each turn of the pedal. This led to the famous high-wheeled bicycle, which looked impressive but was extremely dangerous to ride. Riders sat so high above the ground that falls often caused serious injuries.

Everything changed when someone introduced a chain that connected the pedals to the rear wheel instead of the front one. This clever invention finally allowed both wheels to be the same size. It made the bicycle far more stable and much easier to control. Around the same time, rubber tires filled with air replaced solid ones, making rides noticeably more comfortable on rough roads.

By the end of the 1800s, this safer, chain-driven design had become the standard shape people still recognize today. What began as an unstable wooden toy had finally evolved into reliable transportation used by millions of people around the world.

questions.stem: What was different about the earliest version of the bicycle?
options: It had two chains connecting the wheels.
It had no pedals, so riders pushed the ground with their feet.
It could only be ridden by children.
It had rubber tires filled with air.
optionsZh: 它有兩條鏈條連接輪子。
它沒有踏板，靠雙腳踩地前進。
它裝有充氣的橡膠輪胎。
questions.explanation: 文中 had no pedals at all... pushed the ground with their feet，故選 (B)。

questions.stem: Why did inventors make the front wheel of early pedal bicycles bigger?
options: To make the bicycle look more colorful.
To let the bicycle travel farther with each pedal turn.
To make the bicycle easier to carry.
To reduce the price of the bicycle.
optionsZh: 讓腳踏車看起來更多彩。
讓每踩一圈就能騎得更遠。
questions.explanation: 文中 to allow the bicycle to travel farther with each turn of the pedal，故選 (B)。

questions.stem: What problem did the high-wheeled bicycle cause?
options: It was too heavy to move.
It could not be pedaled at all.
It was dangerous because riders sat very high up.
It had no seat for the rider.
optionsZh: 因騎士坐得很高而十分危險。
questions.explanation: 文中 riders sat so high above the ground that falls often caused serious injuries，故選 (C)。

questions.stem: What change made the bicycle more stable?
options: Adding a chain to connect the pedals to the rear wheel.
optionsZh: 加裝鏈條把踏板連到後輪。
questions.explanation: 文中 a chain that connected the pedals to the rear wheel... making the bicycle far more stable，故選 (A)。

passages.title: Should Students Have Part-Time Jobs? — Two Views
passages.passage:
■ Writer A：
I believe part-time jobs can teach students things that classrooms cannot. When students earn their own money, they learn to plan a budget. They also come to understand the real value of hard work. A student who spends four hours folding shirts at a clothing store quickly appreciates how much effort goes into earning even a small amount of money. Jobs also build confidence. Talking to customers or solving small problems at work teaches communication skills that are hard to practice in a quiet classroom. Of course, students should not work so many hours that their grades suffer, but a few hours a week, done responsibly, can make a student more mature and independent. Parents often worry that part-time jobs waste study time, yet many successful adults say their very first job taught them lessons they still use today.

■ Writer B：
I disagree that young students should take part-time jobs. Their most important task right now is learning, and study time is already limited. A part-time job often means less time for homework, less sleep, and more stress. This can quietly hurt a student's grades over an entire semester. Some students may also feel pressure to accept unfair treatment or low pay. This happens because they are new to the workplace and do not fully know their rights. While learning responsibility is valuable, school clubs and volunteer work can teach similar lessons without taking pay from the hours meant for study. There will be plenty of time to work after graduation. For now, students should focus their limited energy on building a strong foundation for their future.

questions.stem: What does Writer A think about students having part-time jobs?
options: They should never be allowed at all.
They can teach students things classrooms cannot.
They are only good for earning money.
optionsZh: 能教導課堂學不到的事。
questions.explanation: Writer A 開頭即說 part-time jobs can teach students things that classrooms cannot，故選 (B)。

questions.stem: According to Writer B, what problem can a part-time job cause for students?
options: It can hurt their grades over time.
It makes them too strong physically.
It gives them too much free time.
optionsZh: 長期下來可能傷害成績。
讓他們有太多空閒時間。
questions.explanation: Writer B 提到 which can quietly hurt a student's grades over an entire semester，故選 (A)。

questions.stem: What does Writer B suggest students do instead of taking a part-time job?
options: Join school clubs or do volunteer work.
Ask their parents for more money.
Wait until they fail a class.
questions.explanation: 文中 school clubs and volunteer work can teach similar lessons without taking pay from the hours meant for study，故選 (A)。

questions.stem: What do both writers agree on?
options: Phones should be banned at school.
Part-time jobs are related to how students grow and learn.
Students should never take any jobs.
Grades do not matter at all.
optionsZh: 打工和學生的成長學習有關。
questions.explanation: 兩位作者都圍繞打工對學生「成長與學習」的影響來論述，是兩人共同的討論核心，故選 (B)。

questions.stem: Which sentence would Writer A most likely agree with?
options: A few hours of work each week can build confidence.
Students should work full-time instead of studying.
Study time is never affected by jobs.
Only adults should be allowed to earn money.
optionsZh: 每週幾小時的打工能建立自信。
學生應該全職工作而非讀書。
打工完全不影響讀書時間。
questions.explanation: Writer A 主張 a few hours a week, done responsibly, can make a student more mature and independent，與 (A) 一致。

passages.title: Two Weeks That Changed Everything (故事克漏字)
passages.passage:
When Mia arrived at summer camp, she did not know anyone there. She sat alone at the first dinner, __(40)__ everyone around her was already laughing and talking in groups.

On the second day, a girl named Sara sat down next to her and asked if she wanted to be swimming partners. Mia was surprised, because she __(41)__ shy for years and rarely spoke first to new people. Still, she said yes, and the two girls spent the whole afternoon together in the lake.

By the end of the first week, Mia and Sara did almost everything together, from hiking to cooking dinner over the campfire. __(42)__, on the very last night, they promised to write letters to each other after camp ended.

Two years later, they are still best friends. Mia often says that Sara taught her the __(43)__ lesson of camp: sometimes a new friend is only one small question away.

questions.stem: 選出第 40 格最適合的答案。
questions.explanation: 前後語意對比「她獨自坐著」與「大家都在說笑」，用 While（然而／當…時）連接對比子句最合適。

questions.stem: 選出第 41 格最適合的答案。
questions.explanation: 以過去（was surprised）為基準點，「多年來一直害羞」是更早且持續的狀態，須用過去完成式 had been。

questions.stem: 選出第 42 格最適合的答案。
questions.explanation: 承接前文「一整週都膩在一起」，到了最後一晚「終於」互相承諾寫信，故選 Finally。

questions.stem: 選出第 43 格最適合的答案。
questions.explanation: 全文寓意是 Sara 教會 Mia 營隊中「最好的」一課，故選 best。
